destinations.evidence: FHE_PRECOMPILE metadata declares the expected Fhe capability tag.
RPC async admission decodes the expected capability from metadata and rejects executors whose registry capability differs.

equivalence_argument: FHE admission resolves FHE_PRECOMPILE to Capability::Fhe and rejects non-FHE executor capabilities.
notes: The capability check is metadata-driven, so it shares the same precompile capability registry as other async precompiles.
rationale: The commit body explicitly calls out requiring FHE capability, and the validator maps FHE_PRECOMPILE to Capability::Fhe in the recognized precompile routing. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RPC async executor registration validation must require Capability::Fhe for FHE_PRECOMPILE requests rather than skipping capability validation or accepting a different executor capability.
suggested_falsification_tests: Register one executor with Capability::Fhe and another with HttpCall or Llm, then validate identical FHE_PRECOMPILE payloads against each; only the FHE-capable executor should pass validation.
summary: FHE precompile requires FHE executor capability.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Validate an FHE_PRECOMPILE payload against Fhe and HttpCall executors; only the Fhe executor should pass.
title: FHE precompile requires FHE executor capability
